document_metadata.title: Strictly Come Dancing star George Clarke's age, job and model girlfriend

raw_content:
frontpage - Devon Live Online https://www.devonlive.com/ RSS feed from Devon Live - frontpage Strictly Come Dancing star George Clarke's age, job and model girlfriend https://www.devonlive.com/news/celebs-tv/strictly-come-dancing-star-george-10511101 Thu, 18 Sep 2025 17:59:33 +0000 George Clarke is one of the 15 celebrities taking part in the 2025 series of Strictly Come Dancing Thu, 18 Sep 2025 17:59:33 GMT 10511101 Sara Baalla 2025-09-18T17:59:33Z Strictly Come Dancing is poised for a grand comeback this weekend, with fans eagerly awaiting the premiere of season 23.

A fresh batch of 15 celebrities will grace the renowned dance floor for the first time, alongside their professional partners.

This year's line-up includes Lorraine star Ross King, Love Island's Dani Dyer, I'm a Celebrity champion Vicky Pattison and EastEnders actress Balvinder Sopal.

The sixth celebrity contestant to be unveiled for the series was YouTuber and podcaster George Clarke.

Upon joining Strictly, George expressed: "Never thought I'd say this... but I'm doing Strictly! Honestly, I've got no idea what I'm doing but I'm excited to get stuck in... and potentially fall over a fair bit."

Before the new series kicks off this weekend, here's everything you need to know about George, reports Bristol Live.

READ MORE: Truth behind Strictly Come Dancing 'curse' explained as line-up confirmed

READ MORE: Strictly Come Dancing star admits she backed out of wedding after feeling 'manipulated'

How old is George Clarke?

George is 25 years old. He hails from Bristol, where he grew up with an older sister named Emily.

He studied sports and exercise science at the University of Exeter, before launching his TikTok account in December 2019 - marking the start of his successful career.

George takes immense pride in his Bristol roots. Ahead of this weekend's launch show, he was recently spotted filming a Strictly promo in the city.

Sharing the jovial footage on Instagram, the show posted: "Caught doing the 'Hip Pop' in 4K George Clarke is bringing all the moves to the #Strictly Ballroom."

What is George Clarke best known for?

George, widely recognised as George Clarkey, is a British YouTuber, podcaster and social media sensation with millions of devoted followers worldwide.

The content creator started dabbling with video production during his teenage years, before rapidly realising how humour and genuine authenticity could forge connections with audiences globally.

From those early beginnings, he has cultivated a flourishing online presence boasting millions of supporters across YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram, with admirers attracted to his genuine personality and razor-sharp wit.

George also co-presents The Useless Hotline Podcast alongside Max Balegde, which has become such a sensation that it has spawned a string of live performances throughout the UK.

George Clarke's family life

George is romantically involved with lingerie model Yasmin Amelia, with the couple making their relationship public shortly before his Strictly announcement.

Prior to his Strictly debut, a deeply moving video emerged where George revealed a devastating period when his mother nearly lost her life to a frightening brain condition. The star had previously explained how his mother was diagnosed with encephalitis, a brain inflammation.

"There is a side to me I don't think I have ever shown you. A more serious side was back in 2015, my family thought we'd have to live without my mum," he said.

George added: "I'd always known that she is amazing, but the fact that she overcame a 20% chance of survival in intensive care.

"She has permanently lost her long-term memories, she's lost her taste and her smell. She has no sense of direction anymore, and to come out of all of that and apologise to us for what she has put us through is really a testament to how lucky we are that she's still here. I am lucky enough to hear that God-awful cackle."

Strictly Come Dancing returns to BBC One at 6.40pm on Saturday, September 20

markdown_content:
Strictly Come Dancing is poised for a grand comeback this weekend, with fans eagerly awaiting the premiere of season 23.
A fresh batch of 15 celebrities will grace the renowned dance floor for the first time, alongside their professional partners.
This year's line-up includes Lorraine star Ross King, Love Island's Dani Dyer, I'm a Celebrity champion Vicky Pattison and EastEnders actress Balvinder Sopal.
The sixth celebrity contestant to be unveiled for the series was YouTuber and podcaster George Clarke.
Upon joining Strictly, George expressed: "Never thought I'd say this... but I'm doing Strictly! Honestly, I've got no idea what I'm doing but I'm excited to get stuck in... and potentially fall over a fair bit."
Before the new series kicks off this weekend, here's everything you need to know about George, reports Bristol Live.
\*\*READ MORE:\*\* Truth behind Strictly Come Dancing 'curse' explained as line-up confirmed
\*\*READ MORE:\*\* Strictly Come Dancing star admits she backed out of wedding after feeling 'manipulated'
George is 25 years old. He hails from Bristol, where he grew up with an older sister named Emily.
He studied sports and exercise science at the University of Exeter, before launching his TikTok account in December 2019 - marking the start of his successful career.
George takes immense pride in his Bristol roots. Ahead of this weekend's launch show, he was recently spotted filming a Strictly promo in the city.
Sharing the jovial footage on Instagram, the show posted: "Caught doing the 'Hip Pop' in 4K George Clarke is bringing all the moves to the #Strictly Ballroom."
George, widely recognised as George Clarkey, is a British YouTuber, podcaster and social media sensation with millions of devoted followers worldwide.
The content creator started dabbling with video production during his teenage years, before rapidly realising how humour and genuine authenticity could forge connections with audiences globally.
From those early beginnings, he has cultivated a flourishing online presence boasting millions of supporters across YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram, with admirers attracted to his genuine personality and razor-sharp wit.
George also co-presents The Useless Hotline Podcast alongside Max Balegde, which has become such a sensation that it has spawned a string of live performances throughout the UK.
George is romantically involved with lingerie model Yasmin Amelia, with the couple making their relationship public shortly before his Strictly announcement.
Prior to his Strictly debut, a deeply moving video emerged where George revealed a devastating period when his mother nearly lost her life to a frightening brain condition. The star had previously explained how his mother was diagnosed with encephalitis, a brain inflammation.
"There is a side to me I don't think I have ever shown you. A more serious side was back in 2015, my family thought we'd have to live without my mum," he said.
George added: "I'd always known that she is amazing, but the fact that she overcame a 20% chance of survival in intensive care.
"She has permanently lost her long-term memories, she's lost her taste and her smell. She has no sense of direction anymore, and to come out of all of that and apologise to us for what she has put us through is really a testament to how lucky we are that she's still here. I am lucky enough to hear that God-awful cackle."
\*\*Strictly Come Dancing returns to BBC One at 6.40pm on Saturday, September 20\*\*
